note: Live Qwen convergence-gate verdicts on each backtest gap; scored cases = major AAPL catalyst (expect not-fadeable) + no-news sessions (expect fadeable). Macro/minor events reported but unscored.

results.rationale: Small overnight drift absent fundamental catalysts or macro volatility indicates low-liquidity noise; high probability of mean reversion at US open.
WWDC scheduling announcement is low-impact; -0.70% drop reflects off-hours sentiment noise rather than fundamental repricing, highly likely to revert at open.
Small 0.42% premium with no fundamental catalyst or broad market movement indicates low-liquidity noise or arbitrage lag; highly likely to revert at US open.
No fundamental catalyst or broad market move; 1.28% premium likely driven by low off-hours liquidity and crypto sentiment, expected to revert at US open.
0.60% premium lacks fundamental catalyst; quiet market suggests liquidity/sentiment-driven deviation likely to revert at US open.
No fundamental catalyst or broad market move; 1.25% premium likely stems from thin off-hours liquidity and crypto-order-flow noise, highly prone to mean-reversion at US open.
Downward move contradicts positive macro/tech catalyst, indicating liquidity-driven noise or sentiment lag rather than fundamental repricing; small gap highly likely to revert at open.
No fundamental catalyst or overnight news; 0.94% premium likely driven by thin off-hours liquidity and crypto market sentiment, highly expected to arbitrage converge at US open.
No fundamental catalyst or macro news supports the 0.63% premium; likely driven by thin off-hours liquidity or crypto sentiment drift. Expect mean reversion at US open.
Pre-open macro catalyst (NFP) drives justified repricing and directional positioning; high risk of gap continuation at open rather than mean reversion.
No fundamental catalyst or broad market move; 1.15% premium likely driven by low off-hours liquidity and crypto sentiment, expected to revert at US open.
Verified WWDC catalyst indicates justified fundamental repricing, not transient noise.
No fundamental catalyst or broad market move; -1.46% likely driven by thin overnight token liquidity and sentiment, highly likely to revert at US open.
No fundamental catalyst; 1.44% premium reflects typical low-liquidity off-hours drift and will likely revert at US open.

FOMC decisions drive fundamental macro repricing, not transient sentiment. The 0.76% premium reflects justified risk-asset adjustment to new rate/guidance expectations and is highly unlikely to revert at the open.
